questions.q: Who founded the Mauryan Empire?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire was founded by Chandragupta Maurya around 321 BCE.

questions.q: What was the capital of the Mauryan Empire?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire was governed from Pataliputra, near modern Patna.

questions.q: Under which emperor did the Mauryan Empire reach its zenith?
questions.explanation: The empire reached its greatest height under Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: The Mauryan Empire was the first state to unify most of which landmass?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire was the first state to unify most of the Indian subcontinent.

questions.q: Around which century BCE did the Mauryan Empire decline?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire declined in the 2nd century BCE.

questions.q: Approximately when was the Mauryan Empire founded?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta founded the Mauryan Empire around 321 BCE.

questions.q: From which kingdom of the Ganges plain did the Mauryan Empire emerge?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire grew out of Magadha in the Ganges plain.

questions.q: How was the Mauryan Empire administered, according to its description?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire was run from Pataliputra through an elaborate bureaucracy.

questions.q: Which of these emperors belonged to the Mauryan dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bindusara, son of Chandragupta and father of Ashoka, was a Mauryan emperor.

questions.q: What does the emblem of the Mauryan Empire on this card represent its status as?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire is celebrated as India's first great empire to unify most of the subcontinent.

questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor is recorded as 'Amitrochates' by Greek writers?
questions.explanation: Greek sources call Bindusara, the second Mauryan emperor, Amitrochates.

questions.q: Who was the last Mauryan emperor, reportedly assassinated by his commander-in-chief?
questions.explanation: The last Mauryan ruler Brihadratha was killed by his general Pushyamitra Shunga around 185 BCE.

questions.q: Which dynasty succeeded the Mauryas in Magadha after the empire's fall?
questions.explanation: Pushyamitra founded the Shunga dynasty, which succeeded the Mauryas in Magadha.

questions.q: Which Greek ambassador's account is a key source for Mauryan society and administration?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes' Indica, written at the Mauryan court, is a major source on the empire.

questions.q: Which treatise on statecraft is closely associated with Mauryan administration?
questions.explanation: The Arthashastra, attributed to Kautilya, describes the kind of administration linked with the Mauryan state.

questions.q: Ashoka spread his moral edicts across the empire chiefly on what media?
questions.explanation: Ashoka had his edicts inscribed on rocks and polished stone pillars across the Mauryan realm.

questions.q: The conquest of which kingdom prompted Ashoka's turn toward Buddhism?
questions.explanation: The bloody conquest of Kalinga around 261 BCE led Ashoka to embrace Buddhism and dhamma.

questions.q: The Lion Capital from a Mauryan pillar, later India's State Emblem, originally stood at which site?
questions.explanation: The Lion Capital of Sarnath crowned an Ashokan pillar and became independent India's State Emblem.

questions.q: Roughly how long did the Mauryan Empire last from its founding to its end?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan Empire lasted roughly from 321 BCE to about 185 BCE, some 137 years.

questions.q: Which mineral-rich resource of Magadha helped underpin Mauryan military and economic strength?
questions.explanation: Magadha's iron deposits, along with fertile land, helped fuel the rise of Mauryan power.

questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor maintained diplomatic contact with the Hellenistic kingdoms to the west?
questions.explanation: Bindusara kept up diplomatic relations with the Hellenistic Greek kingdoms after his father Chandragupta.

questions.q: Into how many provinces, each with a viceroy, was the Mauryan Empire broadly organized?
questions.explanation: The empire was broadly divided into four great provinces, with centres such as Taxila, Ujjain, Tosali and Suvarnagiri.

questions.q: Which general founded the succeeding dynasty after assassinating the last Mauryan ruler around 185 BCE?
questions.explanation: Pushyamitra Shunga killed Brihadratha and founded the Shunga dynasty around 185 BCE.

questions.q: According to Megasthenes, Mauryan society was divided into how many classes or 'castes'?
questions.explanation: Megasthenes described Indian society of the Mauryan era as divided into seven classes, an oversimplified Greek view.

questions.q: Which official, according to sources, served as a kind of inspector or overseer in Mauryan administration?
questions.explanation: The Mahamatras were high officials, and Ashoka created dhamma-mahamatras to oversee moral welfare.

questions.q: The Sohgaura and Mahasthan inscriptions, among the earliest Mauryan records, deal chiefly with what?
questions.explanation: These early Mauryan inscriptions concern relief measures and the storage of grain against famine.

questions.q: Which Mauryan emperor is associated in Jain tradition with spreading Jainism widely, sometimes confused with Ashoka's legacy?
questions.explanation: Samprati, a later Mauryan, is celebrated in Jain tradition as a great patron of that faith.

questions.q: Mauryan-era punch-marked coins were predominantly made of which metal?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan economy relied heavily on silver punch-marked coins known as karshapanas.

questions.q: The distinctive highly polished Mauryan stone surface seen on pillars is often termed what?
questions.explanation: The lustrous finish on Ashokan pillars is famously called the 'Mauryan polish'.

questions.q: Greek sources record that the Mauryans maintained a large corps of which animals as a key arm of the army?
questions.explanation: War elephants formed a celebrated and feared arm of the Mauryan military, as noted by classical writers.
